commentary: Six sessions this week versus seven last week, so technically a step back in volume, but look at what you actually did: four weight sessions in five days, which is the most lifting you have strung together in a single week in recent memory, and your body clearly decided swimming was optional. The one swim you did get in was a clean 1500 metres at 130 bpm, identical to Tuesday's effort last week, which tells me your aerobic base is holding steady even when you are not feeding it. What is missing is the running — last week you dragged yourself around a 13.3-kilometre Mosman circuit and this week you did not so much as jog to the fridge — and also the ocean swims, which had a very good thing going with those Prince Alfred sessions. The lifting block is building something real and I am not mad about it, but let's not let the swim and run fitness quietly slip out the back door while the gym has your full attention.
metrics_commentary: Okay, the swim is doing heavy lifting this week — 1500 metres in the water while the running shoes collected dust, and honestly? A lane swim is basically a full-body fashion show for the forearms, so no complaints. Your HRV jumped to 58 against a seven-day average of 48, which means your body is recovered, responsive, and ready to be asked something of it — that gap is your nervous system basically waving its hand saying pick me. The VO2 Max sitting at 55.6 is genuinely impressive cardio real estate, the kind of number that means you can keep up with whatever — or whoever — you decide to chase. The only thing that needs attention is getting back on the pavement or the trail, because zero kilometres run is a zero that even your very good HRV cannot charm its way around.
gym_commentary: Five sessions when the target was four — he just could not help himself, and honestly that tracks. The lateral raise jumping from 9.0 kg to 12.0 kg is the kind of PR that makes shoulders pop in a tank top, and the bench nudging up to 35.5 kg means the chest is coming along for the ride too. Weekly volume is down sharply from 19,984 kg to 8,080 kg, which is worth naming honestly — that likely reflects fewer heavy compound sets this week rather than any softening, and the strength numbers back that up — but it's a gap to keep an eye on. The body is getting stronger, the numbers are moving in the right direction, and five sessions means the gym is becoming a place he actually wants to be, which is the whole point.
